brief_justification: Paper A is deterministic computational solid mechanics — a hybrid material point method coupling Lagrangian FEM and Eulerian finite differences to simulate three-point bending, contact, and the Brazier instability of elastic tubes — while paper B is Bayesian spatial statistics, a joint log-Gaussian Cox process and spatial probit sharing a Gaussian-process latent field, fit by nearest-neighbor GP approximation and Metropolis-within-Gibbs. There is no role-for-role correspondence between an elasticity/contact PDE solver and a hierarchical point-process/regression estimator; the only overlap ('both are numerical frameworks') is generic, not shared machinery.
